title: Thermo EASY-nLC + Q Exactive: peptide LC-MS/MS (LC gradient)
description: Thermo nano-LC reversed-phase gradient for bottom-up peptide LC-MS/MS on EASY-Spray PepMap C18 at 200 nL/min (Thermo app note 21550).

payload.description: Reversed-phase nano-LC gradient for tryptic peptides (bottom-up proteomics) on a Thermo EASY-nLC 1200, feeding a Q Exactive Orbitrap. Solvent A is 100% water with 0.1% formic acid; solvent B is 80:20 acetonitrile/water with 0.1% formic acid. Column: EASY-Spray Acclaim PepMap C18, 2 um, 100 A, 75 um i.d. x 75 cm, at 35 C. Flow 200 nL/min (0.0002 mL/min), constant. Injection 1 uL (1 ug HeLa digest plus 100 fmol PRTC on column). Note: this app note showcases an extended gradient on a 75 cm column (5% B held to 420 min at 28% B, then up to 40% and a 95% wash, total 545 min); scale the gradient down proportionally for routine shorter runs. Pair with the matching MS setup template. Source: Thermo Fisher Application Note 21550, https://assets.thermofisher.com/TFS-Assets/CMD/Application-Notes/AN-21550-LC-EASY-Spray-Acclaim-PepMap-C18-75cm-Column-AN21550-EN.pdf . Representative starting-point method, verify against the source and tune per instrument and sample.
